description:
Create a 3D model of three detached tiny homes arranged in a row, and install solar panels on the south-facing roofs.

1. Set model geo-location to Austin, Texas (approx 30.26° N, 97.74° W).
2. Model Unit 1: Draw a 6.0m (East-West) × 4.0m (North-South) footprint and extrude to a 3.0m wall height. Create a Gable Roof with the ridge running East-West, rising 1.5m above the walls (4.5m total peak height). Place its South-West corner exactly at the Origin (0,0,0).
3. Model Units 2 and 3: Create two identical copies of Unit 1. Position Unit 2 to the East of Unit 1 with a 4.0m gap (Starting at X=10.0m). Position Unit 3 to the East of Unit 2 with a 4.0m gap (Starting at X=20.0m).
4. Install Solar: Use the Skelion plugin to install solar panels on the South-facing roof surfaces of ALL three units. Configure the panels to Portrait orientation, standard dimensions, and 0° (flush) tilt.
5. Save the completed model to 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\tiny_home_cluster.skp'.
